title: StressNET: an adaptable deep-learning model for mechanical stress inference in tissues
description:
StressNET is an open-source Python library implementing the deep-learning architecture for inferring mechanical stress fields in biological tissues. It provides training, evaluation, and inference utilities. The software accompanies the associated research article:

StressNET: an adaptable deep-learning model for mechanical stress inference in tissues
Nicolás Aldecoa Rodrigo1,*, Augusto Borges2,*, Jerónimo R. Miranda-Rodriguez3, Guillherme Ventura4, Jakub Sedzinski5,6, Hernán López-Schier7, Osvaldo Chara8,9,10

1) Instituto de Tecnología, Universidad Argentina de la Empresa, Buenos Aires, Argentina
2) Department of Physiology, Development and Neuroscience, University of Cambridge, Cambridge, CB2, UK
3) Instituto de Neurobiología, Universidad Nacional Autónoma de México (UNAM), Boulevard Juriquilla 3001, Juriquilla, México
4) Research Institute for Molecular Pathology (IMP), Vienna, Austria
5) The Novo Nordisk Foundation Center for Stem Cell Medicine (reNEW), University of Copenhagen, Blegdamsvej 3B, 2200 Copenhagen, Denmark
6) Department of Biomedical Sciences, University of Copenhagen, Copenhagen, Denmark
7) Division of Science, New York University Abu Dhabi, Saadiyat Island, United Arab Emirates
8) School of Biosciences, University of Nottingham, Sutton Bonington Campus, Nottingham, LE12, UK
9) Instituto de Tecnología, Universidad Argentina de la Empresa, Buenos Aires, Argentina
10) corresponding author: osvaldo.chara@nottingham.ac.uk
*) These authors contributed equally
